entry.title: Re: Need Basis Expert how to enable configure button on AL11 tcode

this table yes i confirm don't have. so any ide that i can add parameter is it on rz10 i can do it? i also not very sure., i have read on other article but also not sure on that.

entry.title: Re: Enrichment Framework in MDG

is there a possiblility to enrich not only a single entity? For example to update data for BP_CENTRL and AD_POSTAL in the same enrichment.

Currently i am planning to build a workaround via derivation to achieve it, but than the possible functionalities would be restricted.

Best Regards
Tim

entry.title: Re: Need Basis Expert how to enable configure button on AL11 tcode

then I guess that this possibility actually does not exist in your release. I do not know whether adding entries in the instance profile via RZ10 leads to the wanted result, but you can try it.

Jan

entry.title: Re: How to send SAP data to Advanced Event Mesh in 15 minutes, code-less
entry.content:
This has been very helpful, I was only getting key fields populated by /ASADEV/ACI_GEN_PDVIEW_EXTRACT as I had added other fields with "add custom field". But that adds the APPEND_FLAG=X which caused them to avoid being extracted.

I totally missed I was supposed to double-click a table name. Might be nice to have some more explicit buttons on the toolbar to make it more intuitive but OK, now I know this part of the UI.

 

What I am struggling with now, is Join Builder. I am in a position of wanting to specify constants in a join. I have "the old data model". ADRC has key fields ADDRNUMBER, DATE_FROM, NATION.
I want to do

FROM lfa1
INNER JOIN adrc
ON adrc~addrnumber = lfa1~adrnr
AND adrc~date_from = '00010101'
AND adrc~nation = ' '

Can I specify the constant values for DATE_FROM and NATION, to join it correctly?
DATE_FROM is arguably "something we could skip" but NATION is important, we have multiple address versions in use. Perhaps you will tell me to just restrict it with the WHERE?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: How to register Vendor Subranges ?
entry.content:
In the Supplier role of the Business Partner Master, there is "Vendor Subranges" tab, but the New Entry button is inactive and cannot be registered. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to register Vendor Subranges ?
entry.content:
Refer below steps to enable the new entry button in the Vendor subranges tab :

  1. Go to Maintain Business partner app
  2. Open the BP
  3. Select the role as supplier 
  4. Navigate to vendor: general data tab
  5. check the "VSR relevant" flag and "plant relevant" flag in the additional general data
  6. Save your changes

entry.title: Change History for Total Comp shows Original Value of 0 for performancePercent and goalResultsPayout

Hi, 

In our Total Compensation Template, when we open Change history, the values for performancePercent and goalResultsPayoutAmountTotal have original values of zero, even though we have guidelines which sets a default value. These default values are considered as "current value."

Is this the expected behavior? We're seeing this both in our Production and Preview instances.

Note : I have already imported the Script API and Generic API jar from https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#cloudintegration

Best Regards

entry.title: Reconciliation Close Improvements
entry.content:
We have received a lot of feedback on the reconciliation close process. We know the current reconciliation close process has the following flaws, which make it difficult to use.

  1. The current period is by default open. In most cases, the closing activities for the previous period are done at the beginning of the current period. For example, now is September, but you are doing the closing for August. Thus, even though the working period is closed, one can still run matching in the current period, which affects the data in the previous periods.
  2. The lock is at the leading unit level. When a pair is closed, the involved two units are both locked. And all other pairs that involve one of the 2 units are all blocked for matching.
  3. The Reconciliation Close statements of previous periods get changed due to the matching run in the follow-up periods. The historic closed statements should keep unchanged.
  4. The current reconciliation close has no control over the ICMR elimination. No matter closed or not, elimination always reads the latest data from ICADOCM.

We are happy to announce that all above flaws are resolved in the coming releases CE2502 and PCE2025.

Period Initially Closed

By default, periods are initially open, and you need to manually close them at the period end. This setup is convenient if you don't want to block matching until you close the pairs in the current period. However, in most cases, you work for the previous period in the new period. You expect open for the previous period and close for the current period. You can achieve this by checking the "Period Initially Closed" for the reconciliation case that is closed-activated(ICAARC).

Now, when you open the app Manage Reconciliation Close and check the recon case in the new period, you will see the "Reconciliation Close Status" column shows "Initially Closed" and you need to click the button "Open Period" to open all the pairs in the period. If you don't do that, you can still run matching jobs successfully in the period 2024010. However, the matching job only rolls in the new items without generating any assignments. Neither can you manually assign or unassign in the app Manage Assignments.

Lock on Pair Level

Before CE2502 or PCE2025, when a pair is closed, all other pairs that have either the leading or the partner unit will be locked for matching. For example, during a matching run, units A, B, and C are involved. If pair A->B is closed, then B->A, A->C, C->A, C->B, and B->C are all locked against matching.

In CE2502 or PCE2025, when a pair is closed, it only locks the pair; all other pairs are not affected. For example, if pair A->B is closed, then B->A, A->C, C->A, C->B, and B->C are still allowed to match. However, locked against matching doesn't block the data roll-in. New data still gets roll-in to ICADOCM with status "01 Roll-In".

If you run a matching job with some pairs closed and some pairs open, then you will get an application log like below. Bearing in mind that items belong to the locked pairs are not assigned even if they hit certain matching rules.

Snapshot Reconciliation Statement

The reconciliation close statements of previous periods get changed by matching runs in the follow-up periods due to the following reasons:

  1. Journal entries could be posted to the previous posting periods. Thus, the entries are lately rolled in after their posting periods were closed.
  2. Each matching run could un-assign and re-assign assignments that are tagged with the temporary reason codes. Which means reason codes could be changed for certain items, thus affecting the reconciliation statements.

The solution is to snapshot the reconciliation statements when the close action takes place. That is, when the user clicks the button "Close" for certain pairs, the calculated reconciliation statements will persist. The app Manage Reconciliation Close is enhanced to support snapshots.

In the first list view, you can Schedule Reconciliation Close Jobs. Of course, you can still select the pairs and then click the button "Close". However, if the number of pairs exceeds 50, then it will ask you to schedule a job instead. This is because each pair generates and persists a recon statement snapshot, which costs time. If you close more than 50 snapshots at a time in the front-end, it may cause the session timeout.

And you will see 2 amount columns: Difference at Latest Cutoff and Difference at Closing. Since after a pair is closed, new items can still be rolled in during a matching run, thus the amounts can still get changed. The first amount column stands for the latest cutoff amounts (each matching run generates a cutoff timestamp for a leading unit), while the second column stands for the amounts at last closing for the pair. If the 2 amounts are different, then it stands for new data roll-in after last closing, and thus a re-open activity should be considered. If you check the pair C1001 to C1000, you can find the "Reopen Recommended" is set to "Yes".

If you check the details of each pair's reconciliation statement, you can find more information. You can see each side 2 amounts, which are Closing Amount and Latest Amount, with the corresponding cutoff timestamps. Thus, the difference is calculated by adding the amounts from the two sides. You can find in the above picture that the partner's latest amount "-54,509.00EUR" is blue highlighted. This is because after the last closing cutoff, there were new items rolled in, thus changing the summarized amount. You can consider reopening the pair to count in the changes. Or you just leave it as is, then the affective reconciliation closing statement is version 1, as shown in the dropdown list at the upper right corner.

Imagine you run matching or upload to roll-in new items for a closed pair. Then, in the Current Reconciliation page, you will see the affections on the Latest Amounts, Difference at Latest Cutoff, and Unknown Reason category in the Reason Code Breakdown section. However, it doesn't affect the existing reason code breakdown amounts, nor the closing amounts and differences. And you can switch to the historic closing statement snapshots.

Reconciliation Close Control over Elimination

Before CE2502 or PCE2025, the reconciliation close had no control over the ICMR elimination. No matter closed or not, the ICMR elimination just reads what is in ICADOCM up to now. The only impact is that, once closed, data in ICADOCM is frozen. We found our customers somehow accept this, as they don't want too much intertwined between reconciliation close and elimination. They want elimination run without considering too much on reconciliation.

However, having reconciliation close control over elimination will provide the following benefits:

  1. Clear process control makes sure the data consistency and backtracking.
  2. The reconciliation statements go through approval and provide substantiation of the elimination differences.
  3. Elimination status can be monitored in the app Manage Reconciliation Close, which allows to run elimination for dedicate pairs to save time.

In CE2502 or PCE2025, we have made some changes that may not be well reflected on the UI. But I will try to give some elaboration.

Before closing the pairs, you can still run the elimination task (ID 2042). It will generate elimination differences with whatever in ICADOCM. So to say the elimination difference is not substantiated, but you can still get the preliminary elimination results.

Once you closed a pair of 2 consolidation units, there will be a cutoff timestamp recorded on each unit, and the elimination status will be set to "1 Outdated", which indicates the existing elimination result (if it has) may be outdated; a re-run of the elimination for this pair is needed. This time, the elimination run will take the 2 cutoff timestamps to filter in the data from ICADOCM. That is, only the matching items that are before the cutoff timestamp of each unit will be taken into elimination difference calculation.

Since after the pair gets closed, new assignment or un-assignment activity is blocked, therefore, the difference breakdown by reason code is frozen. Although the amounts in the "Unknown Reason" category may still get changed, due to the cutoff timestamp filtering, these changes will not be conveyed into the elimination.

Once the elimination run is finished, the status of the pair is set to "4 Completed". Then, for later elimination runs, this pair will be excluded unless you re-open the pair for any changes.

Now, you can check the elimination difference in the app Group Data Analysis. You will see almost the same differences breakdown by reason codes on the subitem "915", which stands for the transactional difference. And "980" stands for the currency conversion difference; "981" stands for the other difference. It substantiates the elimination differences and makes the data backtracking possible.

Conclusion

We have received a lot of feedback on ICMR. The requirements on reconciliation close are most prominent. We hope the above improvements can clear some obstacles in using the closing features. We will keep listening to your voice from all the channels. We'd appreciate it if you could give more valuable feedback. Together, we can make the product better.

Further Reading

  • The main documentation: https://help.sap.com/s4_ce_icmr and https://help.sap.com/s4_op_icmr
  • Introduction Video: Youtube.
  • FAQ: SAP Note for Cloud(2815332) and OP(2815304).
  • Configuration Guide: https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2916087

entry.title: Error CVIS_UI_GENERIC 011 while creating a BP
entry.content:
Hello,

I am working on a S4H public cloud and I am getting the error CVIS_UI_GENERIC 011 "contact person is a required entry field" while creating the BP in the role "Customer". Why am I getting this error? How can I avoid it?

Thanks and regards

entry.title: Re: Does Flexible Search support for "Select TOP" or Limit?
entry.content:
I prepared groovy script to fetch the records based on starting and total count :-
 
import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.search.FlexibleSearchService
import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.search.SearchResult
import com.saintgobain.core.model.MaterialModel
import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.search.FlexibleSearchQuery
import de.hybris.platform.core.model.product.ProductModel
 
modelService = spring.getBean("modelService")
 
final Map<String, Object> params = new HashMap<String, Object>();
params.put("catalogVersion", "Online");
//Get Products only from Online version
final String query = "SELECT {m.pk} FROM {Product as m JOIN Catalogversion as cv ON {cv.pk}={m.catalogversion} JOIN CategoryProductRelation as rel ON {m:PK} = {rel:target} JOIN Category AS c ON {rel:source} = {c:PK}} WHERE {cv.version}=?catalogVersion"
FlexibleSearchService fss = spring.getBean("flexibleSearchService");
final FlexibleSearchQuery flexibleSearchQuery = new FlexibleSearchQuery(query, params);
//set the starting index
flexibleSearchQuery.setStart(0);
//set the number of records you want to retrive
flexibleSearchQuery.setCount(20)
final SearchResult<ProductModel> searchResult = fss.search(flexibleSearchQuery);
println "Total Products count: "+ searchResult.getTotalCount()
 
for (int rownum = 0; rownum < searchResult.getTotalCount(); rownum++) {
println "Product Code : "+ searchResult.getResult().get(rownum).getCode()
}

entry.title: How to synch data from PA30/PA20 actions of user group to SU01 of BASIS Tcode
entry.content:
I am writing to request your assistance in synchronizing user transfer and resignation data between the Personnel Administration (PA20/PA30) and User Management (SU01) transactions within our SAP system. This synchronization will help us streamline the process of distinguishing and managing SAP users based on their respective organizational sites and departments.

Currently, as part of our user management process, we rely on data maintained in PA20/PA30 to track employee transfers, resignations, and other HR-related changes. However, this information does not automatically reflect in SU01, which is crucial for managing SAP access and ensuring that users are properly aligned with their current roles, locations, and organizational structures.

entry.title: Re: Change History for Total Comp shows Original Value of 0 for performancePercent and goalResultsPa
entry.content: The behavior you're observing, where the original values for performancePercent and goalResultsPayoutAmountTotal are showing as zero in the Change history despite having guidelines that set default values, does seem unusual. Generally, the original values should reflect the default values set by your guidelines, not zero. This could be due to how the system is logging the initial values at the time of creation versus applying the guidelines. I recommend reaching out to your technical support team or vendor for clarification. They might be able to provide insights into whether this behavior is expected or if there is a configuration issue that needs to be addressed.

entry.title: Re: SAP Disclosure Management Stack 2200
entry.content: So you need an 'insider' such as @Marc_Kuipers

entry.title: Re: Round off Values in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi,

You can use formatNumber. Just put .00 in Number Format and you are good to go.

entry.title: System check for Orphaned Client Dependent Data
entry.content:
2618018 - S4TWL - Generic Check for SAP S/4HANA Conversion and Upgrade

 

on this for the below task
Check for orphaned client dependent data

 

1.We have not performed any client deletion on the recent past but need to know how to check the status of any abrupt/stopped Client deletion status at SAP level 
2.Also how to check if any orphaned client dependent data in DB level
3.Also what is impact of this action item "Check for orphaned client dependent data"
It has actioned before conversion ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 2H 2024- LRN-152446 Custom card section location is a...
entry.content: Thank You for responding. Waiting for customer reply to this solution

entry.title: Re: Customized fields at Case management of FSCM
entry.content:
@Shouvick 

Not recommend you change the label for this field SCMG_T_CASE_ATTR-PROCESSOR as it's standard 🙂 maybe you can do this:

  1. Create one Customized field with your new label;
  2. Change the screen layout at the selection screen profile, hide the standard one and show your customized one.

entry.title: Re: System check for Orphaned Client Dependent Data
entry.content:
Hello,

The result of the mentioned check should give you information about the topics covered in your questions. Also, yes, the check should be performed before the conversion.

As mentioned in SAP Note 2399707 - Simplification Item Check

"To learn more about how to perform simplification item checks and the relation to SAP Readiness Check, you can explore the following links:

https://blogs.sap.com/2018/03/26/sap-s4hana-simplification-item-check-how-to-do-it-right./

https://blogs.sap.com/2020/01/02/simplification-item-catalog-simplification-item-check-and-sap-readiness-check-for-sap-s-4hana/ "

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAW 17: Question on JSON parsing (dbo.sp_parse_json p...
entry.content:
In our experience the only way to do this is by using exception handling.

sp_parse_json work for basic scenarios, but as soon as there is any chance of there being variables within the structure we have found it is best to parse with an external procedure

entry.title: Goal Setting: Each goal will have its own unique range and guidelines,the rating should be populated
entry.content:
Customer Requirement: In the goal Setting process, Each goal will have its own unique range and guidelines, and the rating should be automatically populated based on these criteria. These range & guidelines are different for every goal for every employee.

Feasibility: We are using metric lookup table to achieve the rating auto-population. We can define more than one rating scale, based on target, rating scale will auto-populate. However, rating scale & guideline has to be standard which can not vary goal to goal. 

Is there any solution to this query?

entry.title: How to report issue on UI5 control
entry.content:
There is a issue reported by some user in our UI5 app, which is basically issue in the UI5 control.

How do I report the same to UI5 team?

 

BR,

Azhar

entry.title: Printing COA for single batch with multiple handling units
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

There is requirement for our client to print the COA at outbound delivery using single batch and multiple handling units. It is explained as below.

Business creates process order of 1000 TONS, create 1 batch per PO. They confirm the PO partially on daily basis as pallets. 

Day 1: 100 TON, 1 pallet (Pallet is considered as 1 handling unit and multiple handling units are within the pallet). Now in EWM, they can create the inspection lot based on the pallet and result recording and usage decision can be done at pallet level.

Now at the time of delivery, Single batch will be having multiple pallets (Handling units) in it, and all the pallets will be having different inspection results. Now our client wants to print the COA based on the batch (not based on the Handling units) by averaging the results from inspection lots available for Handling units.

Please help me, how this can be handled in standard SAP QM.

Thanks,

Shravan

entry.title: Reporting on Manual Overall Rating
entry.content:
Hi,

I have an issue with reporting on the manual overall rating field of a performance form.  My form (shown in the attached image) has a calculated overall rating (1 in the image) a manual overall form rating (2) in the summary section, and then a manual overall performance rating (3) in the Performance/Potential summary.  However, when I try to report on the forms, I can get the Calculated rating (1) and the overall performance rating (3) but not the overall form rating (2) and my customer needs this.

Regards and thanks in advance,

Geoff

entry.title: Integrating HR Organizational Structure with SAP S/4HANA Flexible Workflow – Possible Solutions?
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts!

Before suggesting further research, please note that I have thoroughly reviewed the SAP Flexible Workflow documentation and searched extensively in the SAP Community, but I couldn’t find any specific discussions on integrating the HR Organizational Structure directly with Flexible Workflow.

However, I did come across solutions related to this topic in the SAP Community forum, but these were specific to classic workflow. You can see an example in this blog: "LINK".

Question:
Is it possible to leverage the existing HR Organizational Structure in SAP S/4HANA to work with Flexible Workflow? Are there any workarounds or integration options available?

Details:

Our organization already has a well-defined HR Organizational Structure that is integrated into our classic SAP workflows. Now, we're exploring SAP S/4HANA Flexible Workflow but have noticed that it relies on "Teams and Responsibilities" rather than directly using the HR Org Structure.

Specifically, I’m looking for insights into the following:

  1. Direct Integration: Is there any way to make the existing HR Org Structure (positions, jobs, and reporting hierarchies) directly usable within Flexible Workflow, so we don’t have to manually recreate elements in Teams and Responsibilities?
  2. Workarounds: Are there any recommended approaches or BAdIs that would allow us to leverage our HR structure indirectly for agent determination or approvals in Flexible Workflow?
  3. Future Development: Has SAP released any recent updates or roadmap information about integrating HR Org Structure with Flexible Workflow, based on user feedback?

Any guidance on achieving a streamlined setup without redundant maintenance of our org structure would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Android Build Error without Log
entry.content: Dear Peter, This issue has been fixed by the product team with the release latest 4.12.108, can you please check and let us know if you need further clarification?

entry.title: no SDBSETUP.EXE in Max DB update form SAP
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

 

the last few updates of the MAXDB and Database Studio didn´t had any SDBSETUP.EXE Files when unpacked...

I downloaded the latest version of the Server Package

When unpacked via sapcar the SDBSETUP ist missing.

Does anyone know if this is a broken release by SAP or if its intended and there is an other way to update MaxDB

entry.title: Configure the SSO between IAS and SuccessFactors(SF) by SAML
entry.content:
Prerequisite:

  1. You have an IAS with the admin account.
  2. You have the permission to access SF provisioning.
  3. The instance can configure the SSO manually. (Newly created instance can't configure SSO manually. They must run SPC process to enable the integration)

Steps:

Go to IAS tenant settings(under Applications & Resources). Choose SAML 2.0 Configuration. Mark the value I marked by red rectangles.

Go to SF provisioning, fill the data as below:

Go back to IAS. Choose Applications:

Create a new SAML application. Keep other values default.

 

Set url as "https://qacand.hcm.ondemand.com/saml2/SAMLAssertionConsumer?company=AFTTeamsM"

Set Name Id to Login Name:

Keep other settings default.

Enable SSO in provisioning. Then try it.

entry.title: Re: Error : SOAP:1023 SRT: Processing error in Internet C...
entry.content: Hi @Raymond_giuseppiconsume the service from a tool such as SoapUI

entry.title: Re: Error : SOAP:1023 SRT: Processing error in Internet C...
entry.content:
Hi @raymond_giuseppi ,

Today I tried consuming the service from SoapUI and i get the yellow highlighted message.

In SOAMANAGER i increased the --> Maximum Wait for WS Consumer <-- time to 360 seconds from 0 which was maintained earlier.

Although the backend processing is taking around 40 seconds to complete, the connection is timing out. 

Any views on this ? 

Thanks

entry.title: Unable to delete the released work order
entry.content:
Hi Team,

When we try to delete the released work orders through the below programs we are unable to delete them:

Preparation program - Set deletion flag / deletion indicator

RIARCORV / RIARCORVS

The WRITE program - Generate the archive file

RIARCORA / RIARCORAS

The DELETION program - Remove the order records from main database tables

RIARCORD / RIARCORDS 

Can you please help me on this.

Regards,

Preethi

entry.title: I am a business user where can I start learning the basics of SAP Concur request?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Getting Started with Concur Professional Request for Business Users learning journey.


Overview
This learning journey is an introduction to Concur Request for Business Users. In this comprehensive journey, you will learn the basics of Concur Request, from creating and working with requests to reviewing and approving them as an approver.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, business users will be able to proficiently manage their requests by using the Concur request platform to submit, approve, and process requests. 

Prerequisites
A general understanding of SAP Concur including Concur Expense and Concur Travel. 

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Python samples in ASE did not work and fail with an I...
entry.content:
I was able to solve the problem: Starting with Python 3.8 the samples must be modified! See
https://www.jamesbowley.co.uk/nbs/ImportError_dll_load_failed_while_importing_cv2.html
From:

import sybpydb

into

import os
os.add_dll_directory(r'c:\SAP\OCS-16_0\dll')
import sybpydb

Where 'c:\SAP\OCS-16_0\dll' is the path for libsybdbcapi64.dll which will be called in sybpydb.pyd.

Hope that helps.

entry.title: Set possible agents for a workitem
entry.content:
Hi to all,

I've the following requirement: 
let's consider the following scenario: a workflow starts for each plant, when a user receives a workitem in his inbox, he must be able to forward it (using the standard forwarding functionality) to another user taken from the list of possible agents who must be taken from an organizational unit depending on the plant.

So, it's not enough to use the "Maintain Agent assignment" functionality for the task since there the organizational unit is static and in my case there should be only one org. unit for each plant. So, it's not a solution to add all needed org. units to the task in "Maintain Agent assignment" because, otherwise, a user could forward the workitem to another user who belong to a different org. unit.

Is there a way to set dynamically the "Possible agents" for a workitem ?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Password change in ECTR
entry.content: Thank you for your reply. I applied kernel 7.85 patch 400 but it won't work. There is an additional note for this issue: 3535378 but it seems to be available only for "SAP KERNEL 9.14 64-BIT UNICODE". Do you think that it will be released for kernel 7.89 or 7.91?

SS

entry.title: Re: Event Processing in an SAP S/4HANA on-premise system
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are using S4 HANA 2023 FP01, and we have noticed that when we want to send out the event "business partner change," we always have to add the event at the highest level. Does anyone have an idea why this is the case?
sap/s4/beh/businesspartner/v1/BusinessPartner/*
sap/s4/beh/businesspartner/v1/BusinessPartner/Created/v1 

Regards Erik

entry.title: Re: To track Urgent orders in App->My Inbox
entry.content:
Hello Dequan Xu,

Thanks for your quick response. Had a quick glimpse of this functionality. 

For our requirement, just to activate only one custom field, any idea which of these options (standard or extended) we can harness?

As per my understanding, if we go by standard template, we can't use the filter options for Custom field in situation types? Right?

In that case, do we need to go for extended option?

Thanks and appreciate your views if any.

entry.title: OData API request not working when Datasphere is set with SSO
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

I have an OData URL that I use to get data from my Analytical Model in Datasphere:

the URL is:  https://tcodataspherefree.eu10.hcs.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/TCO_DATA_MODEL/Landscape_daily_cost_capacity_metering/Landscape_daily_cost_capacity_metering?$top=10

It works just fine if I use it in my browser after I sign in with SSO.

But when I want to call it through a python code, after setting up OAuth on Datasphere, I am not getting any data back from the URL. I assume it is because the SSO is set.

Is there any way to get around that?

Many thanks

Sam

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to export commodity codes from a list...
entry.content: Go to MM60 insert your materials and run the list, change layout and insert Comm. code column to view the commodity codes

entry.title: SD: Trigger billing after GI posting
entry.content:
Hello gurus,

 

we would like to automatically trigger creation of a bill when GI is posted for the delivery.

 

I.e. user creates sales order, creates delivery and posts the GI for the delivery. Now the bill for that delivery is to be created automatically.

 

It seems, SAP CRM does have a feature for this, but is it possible in ECC as well?

 

Thanks
Jens

entry.title: Top 10 from the SAP Cloud ALM October 2024 Update
entry.content:
Top 10 Highlights from the SAP Cloud ALM October 2024 Update

SAP Cloud ALM continues to evolve with new features that enhance project management, monitoring, and traceability. Here are the top 10 highlights from the latest October 2024 update:

1. Mass Assignment of Project Tasks to Quality Gates

The new mass assignment feature allows users to assign multiple project tasks to quality gates effortlessly. This enhancement is particularly useful for managing large-scale projects efficiently.

 

2. Enhanced Overview Cards

Users can perform additional actions directly from the cards on the Home page. These actions include:

  • Editing configuration
  • Navigating to monitoring details
  • Navigating to alerting details

This makes accessing crucial information quicker and easier.

3. Visual Test Execution and Traceability Enhancements

The test execution popover now shows all occurrences of a test case assigned to different test plans. This improvement extends to the Requirement, Solution Process, and User Story Traceability apps, offering comprehensive visibility into test statuses.

4. Critical Subscription Marking

Subscriptions in external API Management can now be marked as "Critical," enabling a self-monitoring capability that proactively identifies and addresses potential issues, enhancing system reliability.

5. Reference Links in Test Cases

Users can add links to external documents and web pages when preparing manual and automated test cases. This feature helps testers access supporting documents like process descriptions and learning materials directly.

6. Comment Integration on Overview Page

A COMMENTS column has been added to the Overview page, allowing users to view the latest comments on issues and standalone actions. This improves communication transparency and issue management efficiency.

7. Quality Gate Status Validation

The status of a quality gate can only be set to Accepted or Conditionally Accepted if the assigned checklist items have valid statuses. Once set, these statuses cannot be changed, ensuring the integrity of the review process.

8. Direct Navigation from Test Case to Requirement Traceability App

Users can now navigate from the test case section in the detail view of a requirement directly to the Requirement Traceability app. This seamless transition enhances traceability and tracking of test execution statuses.

9. Process Hierarchy Nodes Assignment

Test cases can now be assigned to specific process hierarchy nodes, allowing for detailed pinpointing within the process structure and precise process-related test case management.

10. Custom Time Zone for Notifications

Users can specify the time zone for email and chat notifications, customizing the timestamp to align with their local time zone. This personalization ensures more accurate reporting and alerts.

These top 10 features in the October 2024 SAP Cloud ALM update are designed to streamline operations, improve management efficiency, and enhance user experience. For more detailed information, visit the SAP Help Portal.

Stay tuned for more exciting updates as SAP continues to innovate and improve the #Cloud ALM experience

entry.title: Re: Send SAPscript form PDF from output type message to Business Network
entry.content:
Hi ,
How  can we send the attachment directly whenever we save the PO in me21n,me22n,me23n ? I am getting pdf in ariba network when I send it through me9f as own transaction. I need to send it immediately . thanks in advance for help

entry.title: Workflow email notifications being sent in the wrong language
entry.content:
Hello experts

I am configuring the email notifications so the employees can receive them in Portuguese. Under Email Notifications Template Settings I choose the desire notification (Workflow action Approval Notification, for example) and I make sure that the translation to Portuguese is there. 

Then, with the system in Portuguese, the email notification continues to be sent in English. 

Do you know if I am missing some steps on the configuration or what could be the reason of this situation?

Thanks in advance

Regards

entry.title: Re: Results Analysis Method 7, POC Method Based On Project Progress Value Determination
entry.content: For estimation of PoC, T-code CJ20N, tab Progress, click on progress version, then button estimated values.

entry.title: Creating a personal development environment #3 - Vim keybindings
entry.content:
1. #1 - Why Neovim?
  2. #2 - Miscellaneous, status line, and color scheme
  3. #3 - Vim keybindings (this blog post)

Building on top of the previous two blog posts, this blog post will focus learning Vim keybindings, which is very central for using any Vim-based editor, let alone be productive with it.

Before we get started with the keybindings though, let's do a quick recap of what my Neovim configuration, located at ~/.config/nvim/, looked like a this point:

- lua/
  - plugins/
    - filetree.lua
    - theme.lua
  - misc.lua
  - statusline.lua
- init.lua
- lazy-lock.json

What I did so far:

  • configured lazy.nvim as a plugin manager
  • added nvim-tree as a file explorer
  • customized the status line
  • added a nice color scheme
  • other miscellaneous settings

Now while doing all of this, we implicitly already talked about keybindings. After all, Neovim is a keyboard-first editor. You shouldn't really be using the mouse at all (although you can if you want to), which is why you have to use keybindings. However, we only really talked about custom keybindings - the ones that relate to a plugin and where you define the shortcut and the associated operation. But the most important keybindings are the ones that are built into Neovim itself, and since Neovim is a fork of Vim (did I already mention that? 🤔), we can simply refer to them as Vim keybindings (also when googling 😉). These are the ones you will use the most for moving around, switching modes, editing text, and so on. Vim keybindings can seem quite odd at first (from a modern perspective), but they are incredibly powerful and well though-out.

Regarding all my custom keybindings I mentioned that I like to group my keybindings by the category they fall into (in my understanding) and prefix them with the corresponding letter. For example, all keybinding related to the file system start with the letter f, like fe for opening the file explorer. Another example are all bindings related to the language server start with the letter l, like ld for "go to definition". This system helps me remembering them all. It somewhat different to what other people mostly do, which is prefixing all keybindings with a defined leader key, for example space.

Vim keybindings

I will not even try to list or explain the structure of all Vim keybindings in this blog post - others can do that better. I will also not extensively talk about modes, I only really use normal, insert, and visual mode. But I can give you an insight into the keybindings I use on a daily basis and how I personally make sense of them. Hopefully this helps you prioritize which keybindings to learn first and how to remember them. The keybindings listed here work in normal and visual mode, but not in insert mode, where your keystrokes will obviously be interpreted as text input.

For navigating in a file, I use the following keybindings:

  • G: Go to the end of the file
  • gg: Go to the beginning of the file
  • $: Go to the end of the line
  • 0: Go to the beginning of the line
  • e: Move to the end of the current word
  • b: Move to the beginning of the current word

And I do have to confess I am one of the few Vim users who actually uses the arrow keys to move around (I know, I know), instead of h, j, k, and l. I have to reasons for that: First of all, I just couldn't get used to them. Second of all, I often find myself wanting to navigate around in insert mode, and the arrow keys are just more convenient in that situation than switching modes.

For editing text, I use the following keybindings:

  • i: Enter insert mode
  • a: Enter insert mode after the current cursor position (one character further right than with i)
  • A: Enter insert mode at the end of the line
  • u: Undo the last change
  • Ctrl-r: Redo the last change
  • dw: Delete the current word
  • dd: Delete the current line
  • D: Delete from the current cursor position to the end of the line

For selecting text and copy-pasta magic, I use the following keybindings:

  • v: Enter visual mode
  • V: Enter visual line mode (visually select whole line)
  • y: Yank (copy) the selected text (in visual mode)
  • d: Delete the selected text (in visual mode)
  • c: Change the selected text (in visual mode, delete and enter insert mode)
  • p: Paste the yanked text after the cursor

With this set of keybindings (that barely scratches the surface of what's possible) you can actually accomplish a lot of things, especially when combining them. For example, for navigating to the end of the file and going into insert mode at the end of the last line, you can simply use GA. There are probably more efficient ways to get certain things done, but this "minimalistic" set of keybindings works well for me and most importantly, I can remember them. This is just the start and there is a lot more to learn, but that takes time and practice. I will try and add more keybindings to my repertoire as I go along. It's a fun journey.

And that's it for this blog post. Let's talk about the language server and how you can tailor it to your liking next time - arguably the most important and coolest part of a modern development environment.

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Cloud or On-Premise? Not an “either or” option, but a str
entry.content:
Dear @erika_buson thanks a lot for this really useful blog.

In spain i dont see the supported scenario for DRC cloud edition. We are facing in the upcoming year (probably) a new tax regulation in B2B (similar to the Poland scenario)

Supported Business Scenarios | SAP Help Portal

The thing is, we have clients with version ERP 6.0 eph7 no Unicode. And the client is trying to anticipate is they are going to need the integration suite or the DRC cloud edition will be enough to fullfill the needs. Are you aware of this information?

 

Kind regards.

Have you checked KBA 3432140: DI_When using an Add-on you get an unhandled COMException

Please try solution mentioned in this.

Kind Regards
Nidhi

Regards,
Vijey

entry.title: Re: Ensure that you have a database available in space 'd...

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Cloud or On-Premise? Not an “either or” option, but a str
entry.content:
@Jonathan10  the link above has the requirements/scenarios already released. As you mentioned, B2B e-invoices in Spain are future roadmap (there are also rumours of delays, no dates are confirmed). For upcoming regulatory changes, you can refer to regulatory change manager.

Specifically for Spain B2B e-invoices, this is the link and you can refer to the corresponding product impacts tab. You can click/visualise the entry for SAP ERP and you can find the info you are looking for.

We also have a webinar replay, EN and ES. 

Regarding minimum version, you can refer to this page for framework level.

Of course, country-specific pre-requisites can change until development is completed.

Thank you. Erika

entry.title: Re: Professional Service for billing the product P003 is...
entry.content: Hi David, many thanks for your feedback. I'll use P002 for usage based billing.

entry.title: Start learning to develop extensions for SAP Business One, Web client for customer implementations
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Getting Started with Concur Professional Request for Business Users learning journey.


Overview
This learning journey is for developers who want to learn how to develop extensions for SAP Business One, Web client for customer implementations.

Learning objectives
At the end of this learning journey, developers will be able to: 

  • Exploit the options for extending SAP Business One, Web client.     
  • Leverage the SAP Business One Service Layer OData API. 
  • Develop, package, and deploy UI API Extensions for SAP Business One, Web client to customize system views to suit specific business needs. 
  • Build and deploy side-by-side Fiori applications with SAP BTP.

Prerequisites

  • Basic knowledge of SAP Business One and the Web Client. 
  • Recommended learning journey: Exploring SAP Business One, Web Client
  • Basic knowledge of OData and REST API.
  • Basic knowledge of Microsoft Visual Studio Code and JavaScript (Node.js).
  • Basic knowledge of cloud development concepts. 
Record of Achievement
Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: abap2UI5 - (1) Introduction: Developing UI5 Apps Purely in ABAP
entry.content:
HI @Octav ,

Check out the documentation for all the information on how to get started:
https://abap2ui5.github.io/docs/

entry.title: Re: Barcode Scanning function using ABAP
entry.content: Check out all information about barcode scanning with abap2UI5:

entry.title: Re: Barcode Scanning function using ABAP

entry.title: Re: Barcode Scanning function using ABAP
entry.content: Find all information about barcode scanning with abap2UI5: https://abap2ui5.github.io/docs/development/barcodes.html

entry.title: Re: Upgrade : SAPKB70208
entry.content:
Hello,

the warning generated by REV-TRAC tool seems not to be correct if both systems are already on higher release SAP_BASIS 731 and on the same SP level.

SAP_BASIS 731 initial delivery is equivalent with SAP_BASIS 702 SP8, meaning, all objects were delivered in 731 with the same development level as 702 SP8.

It seems the tool compares the installed SPs where the object was delivered and SP8 object list cannot be found on the target system as the object was delivered via export DVDs of release SAP_BASIS 731.

Please contact REV-TRAC and ask for a fix.

Best regards,

Laszlo

entry.title: Re: 403 Forbidden Error when trying to access to the tile...
entry.content: abap2UI5 is successfully tested with S/4 Public Cloud. Find all information about the installation here: https://abap2ui5.github.io/docs/configuration/s4_public_cloud.html

entry.title: Re: Modeling an advanced Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: @prasanna_korada2 take a look at DAC modeling using a hierarchy: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/0afeeed6b56a40f5a02581032ec39420.html?locale=en-US. You cannot use Hierarchy with Directory for this, so you might need to dumb down your hierarchy to fit the more basic feature set of a view with semantic usage "hierarchy". This blog might help you with that: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/build-an-sap-datasphere-parent-child-hierarchy-based-on-s-4hana-hierarchies/ba-p/13497286

how we can send email notification via My inbox Fiorii App ?

entry.title: Re: System check for Orphaned Client Dependent Data
entry.content: It’s interesting how even incomplete client deletion jobs from the past can linger and cause issues later on, especially in a large system environment. Running checks like SCC3 and database analysis through DB02, as well as collaborating with the DBA team, could really help catch these potential pitfalls EdFinancial

entry.title: Re: How to send SAP data to Advanced Event Mesh in 15 minutes, code-less
entry.content:
I'm glad to hear the post helped @loather!
Yes, you can specify constants in your join by using the WHERE condition inside the Payload Designer.
Here's the documentation for more details on how to set up this kind of filtering in Payload Designer: Payload Designer Documentation.

entry.title: Re: Call API returns code 500

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Change Text interpretation runtime is...
entry.content:
Always try it in CR Designer to see if it works, if it doesn't work in CRD it won't work in code.

As soon as you set the text to HTML it automatically left justifies.

This is by design...

entry.title: Re: CPI: Messages are failing due to mapping issue and co...
entry.content: Hi Aamir,
